summary: Publish an honest ScadMill product story, professional manual, architecture guide, and cross-surface public-version contract.

decisions.decision: Treat the landing page, manual, architecture guide, README, application shell title, and release metadata as versioned release surfaces driven by one checked public-version contract. Keep the public site informational only and state that the browser product and WebAssembly engine are not published.
decisions.rationale: A visitor needs a persuasive product explanation without confusing implemented source targets with publicly shipped products. One checked release contract prevents the installer, site, and documentation from quietly diverging.

testEvidence.criterion: Every public product surface names the exact release and the site renders its landing, manual, and architecture routes.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused public-surface contract and the hosting-compatible server-render suite.

red.command: Initial public-surface inventory and first Windows website test
red.outcome: RED: the repository had no public site or unified manual; the starter had generic metadata, a divergent version, and a POSIX-only build command on Windows.

green.command: pnpm.cmd check:public-surfaces && (website) npm.cmd test && npm.cmd audit --audit-level=low
green.outcome: GREEN: 2 cross-surface contract tests and 3 server-render route tests passed; the production build completed on Windows; npm reported zero vulnerabilities after bounded dependency updates.

testEvidence.criterion: The published visitor journey must be reachable, responsive, internally consistent, and bound to the exact supported installer.
testEvidence.test: Inspect all three live routes at desktop and mobile viewports, mechanically follow every route-scoped link, verify live release metadata and GitHub surfaces, and independently verify the already-downloaded public installer.

red.command: Initial post-deploy visitor audit of Sites version 1
red.outcome: RED: the site was live, but the first archive still used a provisional metadata base and the repository and release surfaces did not yet link back to the live product site.

green.command: Post-deploy visitor audit of Sites version 2 at https://scadmill-beta.sconverse.chatgpt.site
green.outcome: GREEN: landing, manual, and architecture pages rendered without horizontal overflow at desktop and 375 px mobile viewports; 53 route-scoped link checks returned 53 reachable destinations and zero broken links; release.json, favicon, social image, rendered GitHub README, repository homepage, and release body were live; the public installer remained exactly 208699552 bytes with the published SHA-256 and a valid Scott Converse signature. Visitor findings closed at 0 blocker / 0 critical / 0 major / 0 minor / 0 nit.

nearMisses.sought: Use the approved hosting-compatible starter only as the minimum deployment scaffold.
nearMisses.confirmation: The generated starter initially carried unused database, authentication, preview, example, and placeholder-brand assets; all were removed before publication.

nearMisses.sought: Confirm the new public site has no reported npm production or development dependency vulnerabilities.
nearMisses.confirmation: The initial starter dependency set reported advisories; bounded direct dependency updates plus fixed PostCSS reached a literal zero-vulnerability npm audit before publication.

questions: No owner decision is required; the owner explicitly directed the implementer to create and publish these launch surfaces.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
